title: verbascum transcaucasicum
intro: Verbascum transcaucasicum, also known as Caucasian mullein, is a perennial plant in the figwort family that is native to the Caucasus region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, making it useful in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions. The health benefits of Verbascum transcaucasicum include relief from coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as improved wound healing and reduced inflammation. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy in various forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used externally to treat skin irritations and wounds.
